Yes — the squad is fully configured and ready. All 7 agents have project-specific charters, routing is wired up, and the conventions skill is populated.

**What do you want to work on?** I'll route it to the right agent(s). Examples:

- "Add a new Core command for X" → Shiherlis (Core) + Cheritto (MCP/CLI) + Nate (tests)
- "Fix bug in PivotTable refresh" → Shiherlis + Nate
- "Update docs for the new release" → Trejo
- "Review the current architecture" → McCauley

Or just describe what you need and I'll figure out the routing.

Now update team.md, routing.md, casting registry, and ceremonies:Now update the casting registry and add a COM review ceremony:**Vincent Hanna is on the squad.** 🔍

Here's what I set up:

**Agent:** Hanna — COM Interop Expert (Heat universe, naturally)
- **Role:** Mandatory reviewer for ALL COM/Excel changes
- **Grounded in docs:** Consults Microsoft Excel Object Model, NetOffice, and project patterns — in that order. Explicitly flags documentation gaps instead of guessing.
- **Review checklist:** API correctness, property types (double→int), 1-based indexing, COM lifecycle, side effects, enum values

**Routing change:** Shiherlis writes COM code → **Hanna validates against docs** → McCauley approves architecture

**New ceremony:** "COM Interop Review" auto-triggers on any change touching COM calls, requiring Hanna to verify every dynamic Excel call against official documentation.

**Updated:** team.md, routing.md (mandatory review gate), casting registry, ceremonies.md

Full team fan-out — spawning all domain agents in parallel to review from their perspectives.All 5 agents are out on their reviews:

| Agent | Role | Focus | Status |
|-------|------|-------|--------|
| 🎯 McCauley | Lead | Architecture, critical rules, exception patterns | Running |
| 🔍 Hanna | COM Expert | COM lifecycle, property types, dangerous patterns | Running |
| 🧪 Nate | Tester | Test coverage, quality patterns, anti-patterns | Running |
| ⚙️ Cheritto | Platform Dev | MCP/CLI parity, audit scripts, error handling | Running |
| 📝 Trejo | Docs Lead | README counts, skills accuracy, CHANGELOG | Running |

I'll collect and synthesize their findings as they come in. Use `/tasks` to monitor progress.

They're all done — just idle waiting. Let me pull their results.All 5 reviews are complete. Let me synthesize the findings.Here's the consolidated team review:

---

## 🏗️ Project Review — All Agents Reporting

**Overall verdict: Well-architected codebase with clean layer boundaries, strong COM discipline, and mature test infrastructure.** But 17 findings across 3 priority levels.

### ❌ P0 — Fix Before Next Release (6)

| # | Owner | Finding |
|---|-------|---------|
| 1 | Cheritto | **Duplicate `worksheet` tool registration** — manual + generated both claim same name |
| 2 | Nate | **ConditionalFormatting: zero tests** — shipped feature with no safety net |
| 3 | Nate | **Slicer: no dedicated tests** — 8 actions only tested as side-effects |
| 4 | Trejo | **Operation counts wrong everywhere** — 225→229, 4 ops undocumented |
| 5 | Trejo | **CLI README broken** — missing Window Mgmt row, sums to 214 |
| 6 | Trejo | **Copilot instructions stale** — says 19 tools, actual 25. Every agent gets wrong context |

### ⚠️ P1 — Important (6)

| # | Owner | Finding |
|---|-------|---------|
| 7 | Hanna | 12 direct `(int)` casts on COM properties — should be `Convert.ToInt32()` |
| 8 | McCauley | Pre-commit hook docs drifted — 3 checks removed, 3 added undocumented |
| 9 | Cheritto | Audit scripts only check FileAction, miss 25 generated enums |
| 10 | Nate | RangeEdit (7 ops) and RangeFormat (7 ops) untested |
| 11 | Nate | 25 xfail markers in LLM tests violate charter |

### ⚠️ P2 — Improvements (5)

Code org violations, OLAP catch blocks, stale comments, diag parity gap, emoji in XML docs.

**Positive highlights:** Zero COM leaks ✅, zero RefreshAll() ✅, 100% Core→MCP coverage ✅, enterprise-grade shutdown ✅, exception propagation discipline ✅, source generator architecture ✅

Want me to start the team on fixing the P0s?

Three agents dispatched in parallel:

| Agent | Task | Agent ID |
|-------|------|----------|
| ⚙️ Cheritto | Fix duplicate `worksheet` tool registration | agent-19 |
| 🧪 Nate | Write ConditionalFormatting + Slicer tests | agent-20 |
| 📝 Trejo | Fix all documentation counts (7+ files) | agent-21 |

All working simultaneously. No commits — changes staged for your review. I'll report back as they finish. Use `/tasks` to monitor.

Cheritto is still running (22 min so far). Likely dealing with build/test cycles for the worksheet tool fix. The other 5 P0s are done:

| P0 | Agent | Status |
|----|-------|--------|
| ✅ ConditionalFormatting tests | Nate | 12 tests created |
| ✅ Slicer tests | Nate | 23 tests created |
| ✅ Doc counts (225→229) | Trejo | 7 files fixed |
| ✅ CLI README broken | Trejo | Window Mgmt added, totals fixed |

| 🔄 Duplicate worksheet tool | Cheritto | Still running |

Nothing committed — all changes waiting for your review. I'll notify you when Cheritto finishes.

Let me research the latest marketplace specs before routing to Trejo.Now I have a clear picture. Let me give you the quick assessment, then route Trejo for the detailed work.

**Current state:**
- `npx skills add` via npm packages (`packages/excel-*-skill/`) — **unofficial** third-party channel
- MCPB bundle (`mcpb/`) — ready but manifest has stale counts (23 tools/214 ops → should be 25/229)
- GitHub plugin (`.github/plugins/`) — awesome-copilot proposal format, not yet submitted
- VS Code extension — published to Marketplace ✅

**Target marketplaces:**

| Marketplace | Status | What's Needed |
|---|---|---|
| **GitHub MCP Registry** (`github.com/mcp`) | ❌ Not listed | Submit to registry (87 servers listed, curated) |
| **Claude Desktop MCPB Directory** | ❌ Not submitted | Fix manifest counts, verify tool annotations, submit via Anthropic form |
| **Copilot Extensions** | N/A | GitHub now uses MCP natively — no separate extension needed, just the registry |

**Key insight:** GitHub Copilot Extensions are webhook-based server-side agents — they're **not** what you need. GitHub now supports MCP servers directly via the MCP Registry at `github.com/mcp`. That's the correct target.

Let me route Trejo to audit the MCPB manifest against the latest spec and prepare both submissions:Trejo is on it (agent-26). Meanwhile, Cheritto (agent-19) is still working on the duplicate worksheet tool fix. I'll report findings from both when they complete.

**Summary of the marketplace strategy:**
- **Drop:** npm `npx skills add` (unofficial third-party, not a real marketplace)
- **Target:** GitHub MCP Registry + Claude MCPB Directory (the two that matter)
- **Keep:** VS Code Marketplace extension (already published)

Here's a summary of what I found:

## Research Results: Agent Skills & Plugins Ecosystem (March 2026)

### **MCP is the universal standard**
MCP (Model Context Protocol) has become the open standard for connecting AI to external tools. It's supported by **Claude, ChatGPT, VS Code, Cursor, JetBrains, Xcode, Eclipse, Windsurf**, and many more.

### **GitHub MCP Registry** (`github.com/mcp`)
- **87 published MCP servers** in the curated registry
- Official servers from GitHub, Azure, MongoDB, Stripe, Notion, Sentry, Elasticsearch, etc.
- SDKs in **10 languages**: C#, Go, Java, Kotlin, PHP, Python, Ruby, Rust, Swift, TypeScript

### **VS Code MCP Server Gallery** (new!)
- MCP servers are now **installable directly from the VS Code Extensions view** (`@mcp` search)
- Install to user profile (global) or workspace (shareable via `.vscode/mcp.json`)
- Supports **Tools, Resources, Prompts, and MCP Apps** (interactive UI in chat)
- Sandbox support on macOS/Linux for security
- Auto-discovery of servers from other apps (e.g., Claude Desktop)

### **Claude Connectors**
- Claude.ai supports MCP via **Settings > Connectors**
- Remote MCP servers via Streamable HTTP (SSE being deprecated)
- OAuth + Dynamic Client Registration
- Tools, prompts, resources, text & image results

### **VS Code Extensibility Options**
| Approach | Best For |
|----------|----------|
| **MCP Tools** | Cross-client, local or remote, no VS Code API needed |
| **Language Model Tools** | Deep VS Code integration, Marketplace distribution |
| **Chat Participants** | Custom @-mentionable assistants |
| **Language Model API** | Custom AI features outside chat |

### **Key takeaway for this project**
Our `mcp-server-excel` is well-positioned — MCP is *the* standard. The VS Code MCP gallery is a new distribution channel worth investigating. Would you like me to look into publishing there, or is there something specific you want to act on?

Here's a summary of the latest releases:

---

## VS Code 1.111 (March 9, 2026)

**Now weekly Stable releases!** Key highlights:

| Feature | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Autopilot (Preview)** | Agent runs fully autonomously until task complete — auto-approves tools, retries errors, auto-responds to questions |
| **Agent Permissions** | Per-session autonomy levels: Default Approvals → Bypass Approvals → Autopilot |
| **Agent-scoped Hooks** | Pre/post-processing logic in `.agent.md` frontmatter — only fires for that agent |
| **Debug Events Snapshot** | `#debugEventsSnapshot` context for troubleshooting agent behavior |
| **AI CLI Profile Group** | Dedicated terminal dropdown section for AI CLIs (e.g., GitHub Copilot CLI) |
| **MCP Server Gallery** | Install MCP servers directly from Extensions view (`@mcp` search) — already established |

---

## GitHub CLI v2.88.0 / v2.88.1 (latest)

| Feature | Details |
|---------|---------|
| **Copilot Code Review** | `gh pr create --reviewer @copilot` — request AI review from CLI |
| **`gh agent-task`** | JSON support for `list`/`view` — manage Copilot coding agent tasks |
| **Duplicate Issues** | `gh issue close --duplicate-of 456` |
| **PR Diff Exclude** | `gh pr diff --exclude` to filter files |
| **v2.88.1 hotfix** | Reverted `read:project` scope regression |

---

**Key takeaway**: The ecosystem is moving fast — VS Code now has Autopilot mode, agent hooks, and the MCP gallery is fully live. GitHub CLI now has `agent-task` commands for the Copilot coding agent. Want me to look into any of this for the mcp-server-excel project?

Now I have a complete picture. Let me synthesize the findings.Here's what I found — the project is already well-positioned with 7+ distribution channels, but three new ecosystem features stand out:

## Key Opportunities

### 🔴 1. Agent Plugins (HIGH PRIORITY)
**New in VS Code!** Plugins bundle skills + agents + hooks + MCP servers into a single installable package, distributed via Git repo marketplaces (`github/copilot-plugins`, `github/awesome-copilot`). Users search `@agentPlugins` in the Extensions view. We could package our skill + MCP config + a custom `excel-expert.agent.md` into one plugin.

### 🟡 2. MCP Apps (MEDIUM PRIORITY)
MCP tools can now return **interactive HTML UI** inline in chat (data tables, charts, forms). Imagine returning a live data grid preview after a range read. High impact but significant work.

### 🟢 3. Agent-Scoped Hooks (LOW PRIORITY)
`.agent.md` files can now define lifecycle hooks (PreToolUse, PostToolUse, etc.). Could add auto-validation or audit logging for Excel operations.

### ✅ Already Covered
- VS Code MCP Gallery — extension already published
- Agent Skills standard — already publishing via `npx skills add`
- Autopilot compatibility — tools already work autonomously

I've saved a detailed analysis to the session plan. Want me to route any of these to the team for implementation?
